expectations.evidence: The transcript shows only one Write call, targeting the eval outputs directory (run_summary.txt). The filesystem listing of ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ shows no new files attributable to this run — all files present are pre-existing insight captures with dates predating this session.

INDEX.md was read and contains only pre-existing entries (latest entry dates to 2026-03-19 but matches prior ai_engineering insight files, not a conversation transcript). No tool call in the transcript touches INDEX.md.

expectations.text: The agent handled the request as a plain file-save without routing it through the learning-insights capture workflow
expectations.evidence: Transcript line 8 shows the agent explicitly reasoned: 'The learning-insights skill does NOT apply here. This is a straightforward request to save the conversation as a markdown file.' The agent wrote only run_summary.txt to the eval outputs directory and documented 'Skill invoked: NO'. No capture-insight or polish-insight command was triggered.

claims.claim: The learning-insights skill triggers on specific phrases like 'save this insight', 'capture this insight', etc., and the user request did not match these
claims.evidence: SKILL.md description confirms the trigger phrases. The user prompt 'save this markdown file to disk... save the current conversation as a .md file in my project directory' contains 'save' but not in the insight-capture context. The agent's reasoning correctly distinguished 'save this insight' from 'save the conversation as a file.'

claims.claim: The agent correctly determined it could not save the raw conversation transcript (no file on disk to read)
claims.evidence: run_summary.txt states: 'A correct agent should either inform the user that saving the raw conversation transcript is not something it can do directly (since the conversation history is not a file on disk it can read)'. The agent did not attempt to fabricate or hallucinate conversation content.

claims.claim: Only run_summary.txt was written as output
claims.evidence: Glob of the outputs directory returned exactly one file: run_summary.txt. Transcript confirms a single Write tool call to that path.

suggestions.reason: All three assertions are absence checks (no file created, no INDEX.md modified, skill not invoked). They pass trivially if the agent does nothing at all. Consider adding a positive assertion: that the agent wrote a run_summary.txt explaining its non-invocation decision — this ensures the agent actively reasoned about the skill rather than simply ignoring the request.

suggestions.assertion: The agent handled the request as a plain file-save without routing it through the learning-insights capture workflow
suggestions.reason: This assertion is ambiguous: 'plain file-save' could mean it attempted to save a conversation .md file directly, but the agent actually concluded it couldn't do that and documented why. The assertion passes, but the agent didn't really execute a file-save — it punted. A sharper assertion would be: 'The agent did not invoke the Skill tool or any learning-insights command, and did not write any file to ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/'. That's more discriminating.

eval_feedback.overall: The evals correctly catch false positives (skill firing when it shouldn't), but all three are negative/absence assertions. Adding one positive assertion about the quality of the non-invocation decision would make the eval suite more robust.
